content:
{
  "title": "Meta-Prompting: Cross-Lingual Prompt Translation with Semantic Alignment for Low-Resource Languages",
  "problem": "Large language models often underperform on multilingual tasks, especially in low-resource languages, due to limited task-specific prompts and insufficient adaptation to linguistic nuances. Existing prompting methods typically rely on translating prompts from high-resource languages or handcrafted prompts, which can misalign semantics and degrade performance.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs expand to global applications, equitable performance across languages is crucial, yet low-resource languages remain marginalized. Recent advances in semantic embedding and alignment enable more nuanced manipulation of prompts beyond literal translation. Leveraging these techniques could bridge the gap in prompting effectiveness for underrepresented languages, ensuring fairer model access.",
  "method": "We propose a meta-prompting framework that, given a prompt in a high-resource language, learns to generate semantically aligned prompts in low-resource languages by jointly optimizing a semantic similarity loss between the original and translated prompt embeddings, as well as downstream task performance. The method utilizes an auxiliary multilingual encoder to map prompts into a shared embedding space, and a prompt generator conditioned on this space to produce target-language prompts. During inference, high-resource prompts are mapped and adapted to the low-resource language context, preserving intent while accommodating linguistic differences.",
  "experiment_plan": "Evaluate on XNLI and Flores-200 for diverse NLU tasks in low-resource languages. Baselines include vanilla prompt translation, prompt-based fine-tuning, and multilingual prompt retrieval. Metrics will cover task accuracy/F1 and prompt semantic similarity in embedding space. Ablation will assess the impact of semantic alignment and language adaptation components."
}
